title: Dont Worry, Its His Sister

jokes.text: And it's all yours, Johnny, it must be a nice feeling, considering you don't have a lot right now. You know, because of the crooked business manager. You know, the money scandal...
jokes.setup: Ray is trying to be congratulatory about Johnny owning the town
jokes.punchline: He immediately reminds Johnny of his financial ruin with tactless enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Classic Ray character beat - his well-meaning but tone-deaf enthusiasm perfectly established

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: Oh, congratulations, Johnny! You own the town now!

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: Of course, you're completely bankrupt and have no money to actually run it, but what a thrill!

jokes.text: Johnny pointing out the suggestive positioning on the town sign
jokes.setup: Johnny sees something inappropriate about the town sign
jokes.punchline: The positioning appears sexually suggestive to Johnny but Ray is oblivious
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's main visual gag - the inappropriate town sign

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Ray, have you seen the town sign? The positioning is... it's rather suggestive, don't you think?

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: Suggestive? It's a town sign, Johnny. It says 'Welcome to Schitt's Creek.'

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, but the way the letters are arranged—

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: It's perfectly standard signage. I don't see anything inappropriate about it.

jokes.text: people come from all over to take a picture with it
jokes.setup: Ray is defending the town sign's popularity
jokes.punchline: He unwittingly confirms people visit for the wrong reasons
jokes.explanation: Ray's innocent confirmation of what Johnny suspects is darkly funny

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: The sign has become a major tourist attraction. People come from all over to take pictures with it.

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: It's put Schitt's Creek on the map. Literally and figuratively.

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: Just last week, a tour bus stopped here. The guide said, 'And here we have the town with the funniest name in America.'

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: I told him, 'Actually, we're known for much more than that.' But they were already taking pictures and laughing.

jokes.text: Oh, I think I see it now. His shoulders are too big.
jokes.setup: Johnny has been trying to get Ray to see the inappropriate imagery
jokes.punchline: Ray completely misses the point and focuses on a trivial detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ray's obliviousness is both endearing and exasperating

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Ray, do you see what I'm pointing at?

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: Yes, I see it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: And?

dialogue.speaker: Ray
dialogue.line: Well, I think your finger is blocking most of the view.

jokes.text: They dare to call me irrelevant? Shag carpeting, that's irrelevant!
jokes.setup: Moira is upset about online criticism
jokes.punchline: She deflects by declaring random objects irrelevant instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - her dramatic deflection and random object comparisons

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You know what? I don't care what people are saying online. They're just jealous. Besides, I have far more important things to worry about. Like the fact that carrots are completely irrelevant. Nobody needs them.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, people definitely need carrots.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I don't. And neither should you. In fact, spoons are irrelevant too. We should all just use our hands like our ancestors did.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's not sanitary.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Sanitary is irrelevant. You know what else is irrelevant? The internet. So I'm simply choosing not to acknowledge it.

jokes.text: Anonymous. Ominous.
jokes.setup: Moira is describing the online commenters
jokes.punchline: She connects 'anonymous' and 'ominous' dramatically
jokes.explanation: Moira's tendency to find dramatic connections between words

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You know, I've noticed that the people leaving these comments are all anonymous. And anonymous... ominous. They're practically the same word.

jokes.text: You mean the hitchhiker that was burning meat over a garbage can?
jokes.setup: Alexis asks about a guy from a party
jokes.punchline: David describes him in the most unflattering way possible
jokes.explanation: David's snobbish description perfectly captures his character and the situation

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So who was that guy at the party?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Which one? The one who looked like he was slowly melting? Or the other one who appeared to be in the early stages of digesting a small animal?

jokes.text: she was face down on the carpet before dragging herself into the closet
jokes.setup: David asks if Alexis has seen their mother
jokes.punchline: Alexis casually describes Moira's dramatic breakdown
jokes.explanation: Alexis's casual tone about their mother's dramatic behavior is perfectly absurd

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Have you seen Mom?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, yes. She's in the living room having a complete meltdown. Like, full Moira meltdown. She's on the fainting couch, there's a silk scarf involved, and I'm pretty sure she's quoting Sunset Boulevard.

jokes.text: I thought she was maybe looking for a contact or something
jokes.setup: David is shocked Alexis didn't check on Moira
jokes.punchline: Alexis's innocent explanation for ignoring obvious distress
jokes.explanation: Alexis's obliviousness to family dysfunction is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I didn't think it was that serious. I mean, she was still wearing her wig.

jokes.text: No, I did Saint-Tropez, so. And I did her birthday, and Aspen twice.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks them to take care of Moira
jokes.punchline: They treat caring for their mother like a chore rotation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - they've systematized dealing with their mother's breakdowns

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Okay, so like, Monday through Wednesday is me?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes. Thursday through Saturday is mine.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: And Sunday?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: We both take Sunday off.

jokes.text: my horoscope said that I shouldn't assume responsibility for anybody but myself today
jokes.setup: Johnny insists they help their mother
jokes.punchline: Alexis uses her horoscope as an excuse to avoid responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis - using pseudo-spirituality to avoid family obligations

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We need to help your mother with this.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I can't, my horoscope said today is not a good day for taking on new responsibilities.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Alexis, you can't use your horoscope as an excuse to avoid everything.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not going to argue with the universe, Dad.

jokes.text: Yeah, and my horoscope says, why don't you go in and help your mother!
jokes.setup: Alexis uses horoscope as excuse
jokes.punchline: Johnny sarcastically creates his own horoscope command
jokes.explanation: Johnny's exasperated sarcasm works well as a comeback

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, let me just consult my horoscope. Oh, it says here that today I'm going to encounter a stubborn Pisces who won't listen to reason. How convenient.

jokes.text: Though it is kind of fun to watch, though. Not when you're the one she's trying to back over with a car.
jokes.setup: Alexis admits Moira's breakdowns are entertaining
jokes.punchline: David reveals a specific dangerous incident
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that reveals the family's dysfunction while staying funny

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, they're kind of entertaining.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, she drove a golf cart through the front window of the Café Tropical.

jokes.text: I don't think you understand, I already have it.
jokes.setup: Johnny explains David needs to pay for expensive cream
jokes.punchline: David's privileged logic - possession negates payment obligation
jokes.explanation: Perfect David - his entitled worldview where ownership supersedes economics

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David, you need to pay for that expensive cream.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I have it now, so clearly I don't need to pay for it.

jokes.text: You want the smooth under eyes of a 16-year-old, get a job!
jokes.setup: David keeps complaining about needing expensive cream
jokes.punchline: Johnny's exasperated summary of the situation
jokes.explanation: Johnny's frustrated attempt to inject reality into David's priorities

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: So you need an expensive cream for your face, and you don't have any money. That's the situation.

jokes.text: What kind of name is Kika anyway?
jokes.setup: Alexis mentions her boyfriend left her for a flight attendant
jokes.punchline: She focuses on criticizing the other woman's name
jokes.explanation: Alexis deflecting hurt by attacking superficial details

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: He left me for a flight attendant.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Her name is Krystal. With a K. And a Y. And no second L.

jokes.text: You were a couple of doe eyed lovers aboard a trans-Atlantic airship filled with gas!
jokes.setup: Moira is trying to comfort Alexis about her breakup
jokes.punchline: Her overly dramatic and oddly specific romantic description
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Moira - overwrought romanticism with bizarre specificity

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Love is like a beautiful, exotic bird that lands on your shoulder, and you think it's going to stay forever, but then it flies away and poops on your Valentino.

jokes.text: (Snorts like a pig) You date pigs.
jokes.setup: Moira and Alexis are having a mother-daughter conversation
jokes.punchline: Moira makes pig noises then insults Alexis's dating choices
jokes.explanation: Moira's theatrical cruelty combined with childish sound effects

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oink oink oink!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alexis, your taste in men is absolutely abysmal.

jokes.text: I just remembered that being a mother is not your strong suit.
jokes.setup: Moira is being insulting while intoxicated
jokes.punchline: Alexis's dry observation about Moira's parenting
jokes.explanation: Alexis's matter-of-fact delivery of harsh family truth

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, this explains so much about my childhood.

jokes.text: I was hungry! But I could still eat.
jokes.setup: Alexis accuses Moira of taking too many anti-depressants
jokes.punchline: Moira's bizarre logic about eating pills then still wanting food
jokes.explanation: Moira's twisted logic about medication and appetite

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, if I take an anti-depressant, I'm still going to want to eat. The two are not mutually exclusive.

jokes.text: Um, something in like, art curating, or trend forecasting.
jokes.setup: Stevie asks what kind of job David wants
jokes.punchline: David's completely unrealistic expectations for rural employment
jokes.explanation: David's disconnect between his skills and current reality

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: What kind of job are you looking for?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I was thinking something in the fashion industry. Or perhaps luxury goods. I have a very specific set of skills.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: David, we're in Schitt's Creek.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, and I'm sure there's a thriving high-end retail market here. Or at minimum, a Hermès boutique.

jokes.text: Not seeing anything in art curating, or trend forecasting, that's weird.
jokes.setup: David lists his preferred job types
jokes.punchline: Stevie's dry sarcasm about the lack of such jobs in Schitt's Creek
jokes.explanation: Stevie's perfect deadpan response to David's unrealistic expectations

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I prefer jobs that are well-compensated, intellectually stimulating, and don't require me to interact with the general public.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: So you're looking for a job that doesn't exist in Schitt's Creek. Or anywhere.

jokes.text: Which is what, forty, forty-five something an hour?
jokes.setup: Stevie mentions minimum wage
jokes.punchline: David's wildly inflated assumption about minimum wage rates
jokes.explanation: David's complete disconnect from economic reality

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Minimum wage? I assumed that was at least $47 an hour.

jokes.text: Mmm-hmm, exactly.
jokes.setup: David guesses minimum wage is $40-45 an hour
jokes.punchline: Stevie's deadpan agreement rather than correcting him
jokes.explanation: Stevie's choice to let David maintain his delusion is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I would say minimum wage is probably somewhere in the $40 to $45 an hour range.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, that sounds about right.

jokes.text: she will have scrambled egg whites and some steamed spinach, please
jokes.setup: Moira orders indulgent comfort food
jokes.punchline: Alexis immediately orders healthy food for her instead
jokes.explanation: Alexis taking control of her mother's diet choices

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm going to have the lobster thermidor, a Caesar salad with extra dressing, and the chocolate lava cake.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Actually, Mom, I think you should get the grilled salmon, a side salad with dressing on the side, and maybe just some fresh fruit.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alexis, I didn't ask for your input.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know, but you're going to thank me later.

jokes.text: Oh wow, I'm really bored.
jokes.setup: Alexis is supposed to be watching Moira
jokes.punchline: She immediately abandons her responsibility due to boredom
jokes.explanation: Alexis's complete inability to handle responsibility

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm just going to step outside for a second.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Where are you going? You're supposed to be watching me.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know, but like, you're just sitting there. I'll be right back.

jokes.text: Are you, like, a poor person, or?
jokes.setup: Mutt explains he takes food scraps for composting
jokes.punchline: Alexis's tone-deaf question about poverty
jokes.explanation: Alexis's privileged worldview creates perfect cringe comedy

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're telling me you're taking our garbage?

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: It's not garbage, it's compost. It breaks down into nutrient-rich soil.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Right, okay. So... do poor people do this?

jokes.text: Gwyneth Paltrow does a compost gift exchange.
jokes.setup: Mutt mentions composting
jokes.punchline: Alexis relates it to celebrity lifestyle trends
jokes.explanation: Alexis connecting everything to celebrity culture she knows

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, composting? That's like, so in right now. All the A-listers in LA are doing it. It's basically just... trash yoga.

jokes.text: and hoping the building will collapse.
jokes.setup: Someone asks how Moira is doing
jokes.punchline: Her dramatically dark and specific wish
jokes.explanation: Moira's theatrical despair expressed through architectural destruction

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm doing well, thank you for asking. Though I must confess, I've been having the most morbid thought. I've decided that when I die, I want to be cremated and have my ashes mixed into a luxury skincare line. That way, I'll finally have a product that's truly one-of-a-kind and will make people's skin absolutely radiant. It's the only way I can guarantee my legacy will be universally revered.

jokes.text: That must have taken such courage to ask me that.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn asks Moira to help with a school play
jokes.punchline: Moira treats the simple request as a brave act
jokes.explanation: Moira's inflated sense of her own importance and intimidation factor

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Moira, would you help me with the school play?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Jocelyn, darling, I'm deeply moved by your courage. To ask me, knowing full well that I could overshadow every performance with my mere presence? It's a selfless act of bravery.

jokes.text: That's all that I'm apparently qualified to be.
jokes.setup: David reports he has an interview to be a bag boy
jokes.punchline: His dramatic self-pity about his limited options
jokes.explanation: David's wounded pride about his reduced circumstances

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I have an interview to be a bag boy.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I mean, what have I become? I have a business degree from a private university, I've managed multi-million dollar accounts, and now I'm going to be placing groceries into bags for people.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: This is my life now. This is what I've been reduced to.

jokes.text: I'm interviewing to be a bag boy, not a personal injury lawyer.
jokes.setup: Johnny says the interview is important for first impressions
jokes.punchline: David's dismissive comparison minimizing the job's importance
jokes.explanation: David's snobbish attitude toward 'lesser' employment

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: The interview is important for first impressions.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's not like we're performing open heart surgery.

jokes.text: I ran out of eye cream.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks again why David wants the job
jokes.punchline: David's honest but inappropriate motivation
jokes.explanation: Callbacks to David's vanity and the episode's setup

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Because I need to prove to everyone that I'm not just some useless trust fund kid who can't do anything on his own. Also, I'm deeply competitive and can't stand the idea of someone else being better at something than me. And frankly, the salary would really help with my skincare routine.

jokes.text: Colostomy bag pants!
jokes.setup: Johnny is critiquing David's interview outfit
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific and harsh description of David's fashion
jokes.explanation: Johnny's frustrated outburst creates unexpectedly specific and quotable insult

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That is not an interview outfit. That is a outfit for someone who is going to sexually assault a beet.

jokes.text: Raise your swords!
jokes.setup: Moira asks kids about theater background
jokes.punchline: She uses theatrical language inappropriately with children
jokes.explanation: Moira's theatrical intensity completely inappropriate for elementary students

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: So children, tell me about your theatrical background. Have you studied the craft?

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: Um, we did a play at school.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, naturally. And did you find yourself fully inhabiting the emotional subtext of your character's journey?

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: I was a tree.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Ah, yes. A decidedly avant-garde choice. Tell me, how did you approach the arboreal vulnerability of your role?

jokes.text: We're not here to play, or to have fun!
jokes.setup: Moira is working with elementary school children
jokes.punchline: She applies professional theater intensity to kids' activities
jokes.explanation: Moira completely missing the point of children's theater

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Children, gather 'round. We are not simply playing a game. We are channeling the raw, unbridled energy of the human spirit.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I need you to access your inner Meisner. Yes, even you, Bradley. Especially you, Bradley.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This finger painting is not a craft activity. It is a visceral exploration of color, form, and existential dread.

jokes.text: It's not your fault, the script is garbage!
jokes.setup: Kids are struggling with their lines in the drug awareness play
jokes.punchline: Moira blames the script written by their teacher
jokes.explanation: Moira's brutal honesty about Jocelyn's writing in front of everyone

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not surprised the children are struggling. Have you read this script? It's pedestrian drivel. The dialogue is wooden, the subtext is nonexistent, and the character arcs are flatter than my hair on a humid day. I wouldn't be caught dead performing this material, and frankly, I'm appalled that a supposed educator thought this was acceptable.

jokes.text: The quiet suffering of a woman trapped in a relationship with a simpleton.
jokes.setup: Moira suggests Jocelyn write what she knows
jokes.punchline: She cruelly describes Jocelyn's marriage to Roland
jokes.explanation: Brutally cutting observation delivered with theatrical authority - perfectly crafted insult

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Write what you know.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Oh, that's good advice.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, you could write about your marriage to Roland. Now there's a tragedy in three acts.

jokes.text: (Shouting) What is it you need?
jokes.setup: Moira is trying to coach a child actor
jokes.punchline: She escalates to shouting at a small child during drug awareness play
jokes.explanation: Moira's inappropriate intensity with children creates cringe comedy

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: No, no, no. You need to find the emotional core of this moment. The fear, the vulnerability of a child confronted with peer pressure.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Again. And this time, really feel it.

dialogue.speaker: Child Actor
dialogue.line: Um... I don't want to do drugs?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: LOUDER! YOUR MOTHER IS CRYING! YOUR FATHER HAS ABANDONED YOU! THE DRUGS ARE WINNING!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Now, do you see what you did there? You found it.

jokes.text: He discovered this land, he developed it, and he turned it into the little slice of heaven that it is.
jokes.setup: Roland is defending his great-grandfather's legacy
jokes.punchline: He describes the clearly dysfunctional town as a 'slice of heaven'
jokes.explanation: Roland's blind loyalty to a place that clearly has problems

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: My great-grandfather founded this town, and it's a slice of heaven.

jokes.text: It looks like your great grandfather's (Bleep) your great grandmother right up the ass!
jokes.setup: Johnny finally explains exactly what he sees in the town sign
jokes.punchline: His explosive, crude description of the visual imagery
jokes.explanation: Johnny's breaking point creates the episode's biggest laugh - explosive frustration

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Look, I know what you all think when you look at that sign. But let me tell you exactly what I see. I see a giant, gaping... well, it looks like a massive pair of... you know what? It's a town sign that appears to have been designed by someone with absolutely no understanding of visual composition or basic human decency. The way those letters are positioned, the spacing, the overall offensive nature of the imagery — it's like someone deliberately tried to make it look obscene. And frankly, I think that's exactly what happened here.

jokes.text: That isn't even Horace's wife! That's his sister.
jokes.setup: Johnny is horrified by the sexual imagery
jokes.punchline: Roland's correction makes it much worse, not better
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - the 'correction' makes everything infinitely worse. Episode title joke.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, I'm not sure this is appropriate imagery for a town festival.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: What? That's not a... that's a cornucopia.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It looks like—

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: It's overflowing with produce.

jokes.text: we don't do that sort of thing with our sisters!
jokes.setup: Roland is trying to defend his family's honor
jokes.punchline: His defensive statement implies he thinks that's the real issue
jokes.explanation: Roland completely missing that the visual problem isn't about family dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, I'll have you know that my family is not poor!

jokes.text: The opportunities will diminish, and the ass will get bigger, oh, you can bet your bottom dollar it will! Especially yours. You're going to have a huge ass.
jokes.setup: Moira is giving life advice to children
jokes.punchline: She gets increasingly dark and personal with a specific child
jokes.explanation: Moira's complete breakdown of appropriate boundaries with children

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Children, life is a journey. You must always be true to yourself and follow your dreams.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: However, sometimes life will disappoint you. Sometimes the people you love will leave you. Sometimes you'll make choices you deeply regret.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: And sometimes, you'll look in the mirror and not recognize the person staring back at you. Isn't that right... what's your name again?

jokes.text: And you, future baldy. Sorry, hairless, homeless!
jokes.setup: Moira continues her inappropriate life predictions
jokes.punchline: She gets even more cruel and specific with another child
jokes.explanation: Moira's complete abandonment of child-appropriate behavior

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You, little one, will grow up to be a moderately successful insurance adjuster in a strip mall outside of Poughkeepsie, where you'll spend your evenings arguing with your spouse about whose turn it is to take out the recycling.

jokes.text: This is worse. This is much worse!
jokes.setup: Johnny sees Roland's 'fix' to the town sign
jokes.punchline: The modification has made the imagery even more inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - the 'solution' creates an even bigger problem

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What did Roland do to the sign?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, I noticed the town symbol looked a little... suggestive, so I added a leaf to cover it up.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, that's not a leaf. That's a... that's worse.

jokes.text: This is worse. This is much worse!
jokes.setup: Johnny asked Roland to fix the inappropriate sign
jokes.punchline: The 'fix' has made the sexual imagery even more obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback escalation - the solution creates an even bigger problem

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, what have you done? This is worse than before!

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, I tried to cover it up, but I guess I just made it look more...

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: More what, Roland? More suggestive? More inappropriate?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I was going to say 'creative.'

tmdb_overview: Johnny tries to get a potentially offensive town sign taken down, while David is faced with the frightening task of finding a job.
insight: Rose's obliviousness drives 18 of 47 jokes—her character comedy single-handedly lifts this episode to elite tier.
